name: Jansson's Frestelse (Jansson's Temptation)
recipeIngredient: 7 medium Boiling potatoes, peeled & cut into 2-inch X 1/4-inch strips
2 large (to 3) yellow onions, thinly sliced (4 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Place the potato strips in cold water to keep them from discoloring.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons of butter and 2 tablespoons of oil in a 10-12 inch skillet; when the foam subsides, add the onions and cook for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, until they are soft but not brown.
4. Spread the remaining half tablespoon of butter on a quart-sized souffle or baking dish lined with parchment paper.
5. Drain the potatoes and pat them dry with paper towels. Arrange a layer of potatoes at the bottom of the dish and then alternate layers of onions and anchovies, ending with potatoes. Sprinkle each layer with a little white pepper.
6. Scatter bread crumbs over the top layer of potatoes and dot the casserole with the 2 tablespoons of butter cut into bits.
7. In a small saucepan, heat the milk and cream until it barely simmers, then pour over the potatoes.
8. Bake in the center of the oven for 45 minutes or until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a sharp knife or skewer and the liquid is nearly absorbed.
